title: erigeron alpinus subsp. neglectus
intro: Erigeron alpinus subsp. neglectus, also known as the Alpine Fleabane or Alpine Daisy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Erigeron alpinus subsp. neglectus include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight infections, and promote healing, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various ailments. Erigeron alpinus subsp. neglectus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
